title: Extraction of the SecinH3 from mouse liver
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"potmi","text":"Mice fed with the cytohesin inhibitor SecinH3 for two days develop hepatic insulin resistance that can be identified by reduced liver glycogen levels, increased serum insulin and ketone body levels and decreased serum non-esterified fatty acid. To confirm the presence and identity of SecinH3 in mouse liver, we extracted the compound from liver homogenates with chloroform and identified it by LC/MS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 300 mg mouse liver each weighed into 15 mL centrifugation tubes and taken up in 1 ml PBS, 2 mL chloroform added.
Incubated at RT for 30' under agitation.
Chloroform phase transferred into fresh centrifugation tube.
Water phase extracted second time on shaker with 1.5 mL chloroform, 20'.
Chloroform phases combined and solvent removed on SpeedVac.
Residue dissolved in 100 µL/100 mg liver methanol, centrifuged 5' at 14.000 rpm and liquid transferred into HPLC glass vial.

Fractionated via HPLC on Nucleosil RP18 material 5 µm (10x4.0 mm column); gradient: 0-7 min: 70% water, 30% methanol 7-15 min: 40% water, 60% methanol 15-20 min: 100% methanol; fractions 9-12 min collected.
Solvent of collected fractions removed by SpeedVac and residue taken up in 50 µl methanol.
Fractionated by HPLC, column: MultoHigh RP18 5 µM (125x2.0 mm column); gradient: 0 min: 50% water, 50% methanol 5 min: 50% water, 50% methanol 25 min: 10% water, 90% methanol 25.1 min: 100% methanol.
SecinH3 elutes at ca. 20 min; analyzed by MS.
Inhibition of cytohesins by SecinH3 leads to hepatic insulin resistance by Hafner, M. et al. published in Nature on 17 Nov, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature05415
